documents.path: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/FINPROVE stitching & segmentation assessment notes.pdf
documents.name: FINPROVE stitching & segmentation assessment notes.pdf
documents.excerpt:
Stitching 
 
Kuvat powerpointissa 
Muuta huomioitavaa 
 
_p2 : adenocarcinoma vagina 
• Eka vs vika sykli muutamia irtonaisia soluja 
• Heilahtanut jossain kohdassa 
• Taustaa oikea ala kulma & markkerit napannut tähän voimakkaasti -> tod. näk. 
verisoluja ja verenvuotoa -> poista QC:ssä 
• Blurry spots vähän etenkin kanava 0 DAPI -> poista QC:ssä 
• Vasen ala kulma vasta -aine värjäys levinnyt re

Is downstream of MKK’s, which in turn can be activated by members of the Ras family, leading to an additional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway. JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.

vector_chunks.text:
Chosen targets circled in red.

Erk is the main kinase in the MAPK cascade, and in order to quantify the overall activity of the MAPK pathway we need to assess the level of Erk activity. Additionally, the ab is available directly conjugated, so we do not use up a non-conjugated 1st cycle slot with pErk.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text: CREB2, i.e. ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g. PI3K-Akt-pathway. Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text: CREB2, i.e. ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g. PI3K-Akt-pathway. Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text: JNK is a kinase downstream for Ras, i.e. activated by components upstream of MEKi, and leads to antiapoptotic signaling. Quantifying JNK activity can give insights as to why MEKi does not lead to improved survival, as cancer cells potentially rely on other pathways to survive instead of the canonical Erk driven MAPK pathway, activated by factors upstream of MEKi, and thus leave the cancer cells able to survive MEKi.

Nanostring panel & tCycIF imaging on patients with advanced gynecological malignancies in the FINPROVE clinical trial. Special focus on patients treated with MEK inhibitors & LGSC patients. Our aim is to discover correlations between transcriptional signatures, spatial protein expression and treatment responses.

R41: Jätetty pois markkerit jotka on dropattu: CD206, pJNK
R43: Cell type calling kannattaa yleensä tehä silleen et eka jakaa solut isompiin ryhmiin
R44: (esim tuumori, strooma, myeloid, lymphoid), ja sit vasta nää alaryhmiin esim myeloid -> ja sit lymphoid ->
R45: makrofagit usein jostain syystä ekspressoi CD4 et sitä ei kannata laittaa niihin negatiiviseks
R46: ja toinen juttu mitä sanoinki jo et osa CD8+T-soluista ekspressoi NKG2A niin sitä ei kannata laittaa niihin negatiiviseks vaikka onkin periaatteessa NK-markkeri

FINPROVE is Finland’s first multi-center, non-randomized phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA-approved targeted therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who have exhausted standard treatments. Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper molecular insight.

FINPROVE is Finland’s first multi-center, non-randomized phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA-approved targeted therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who have exhausted standard treatments. Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper molecular insight.

Between 1/2022 and 4/2025, tumors of 765 patients underwent targeted next-generation sequencing (NGS), of which 103 (13%) were gynecological cancers. NGS data were analyzed to identify potentially actionable alterations. A subset of tumors underwent further characterization using NanoString gene expression profiling and single-cell spatial proteomics. To date, 17 samples have been analyzed with tissue cyclic immunofluorescence (tCycIF) and 12 with NanoString nCounter profiling, aiming for a cohort of at least 36 patients.

Between 1/2022 and 4/2025, tumors of 765 patients underwent targeted next-generation sequencing (NGS), of which 103 (13%) were gynecological cancers. NGS data were analyzed to identify potentially actionable alterations. A subset of tumors underwent further characterization using NanoString gene expression profiling and single-cell spatial proteomics. To date, 17 samples have been analyzed with tissue cyclic immunofluorescence (tCycIF) and 12 with NanoString nCounter profiling, aiming for a cohort of at least 36 patients.

The most frequent gynecological cancers were high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC, n=28), low-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (LGSC, n=14) and uterine leiomyosarcoma (n=9). The most frequent targetable alterations were in KRAS (n=18) and BRAF (n=3). At data cutoff, 28 patients had received targeted treatment with a median treatment duration of 4 months. Among them, 1 complete response, 9 partial responses, 8 stable diseases, and 10 progressions were observed resulting in a disease control rate of 64% at 16 weeks, the trial’s primary endpoint. Preliminary NanoString data revealed differential MAPK pathway gene expression correlating with treatment responses. Moreover, expression of interferon-related genes correlated with response duration. The tCycIF analyses revealed distinct spatial MAPK pathway activation patterns across histologies, with a prominent activation on the tumor boundaries in LGSC.

The most frequent gynecological cancers were high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC, n=28), low-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (LGSC, n=14) and uterine leiomyosarcoma (n=9). The most frequent targetable alterations were in KRAS (n=18) and BRAF (n=3). At data cutoff, 28 patients had received targeted treatment with a median treatment duration of 4 months. Among them, 1 complete response, 9 partial responses, 8 stable diseases, and 10 progressions were observed resulting in a disease control rate of 64% at 16 weeks, the trial’s primary endpoint. Preliminary NanoString data revealed differential MAPK pathway gene expression correlating with treatment responses. Moreover, expression of interferon-related genes correlated with response duration. The tCycIF analyses revealed distinct spatial MAPK pathway activation patterns across histologies, with a prominent activation on the tumor boundaries in LGSC.

Frequent KRAS and BRAF alterations were identified in gynecological cancers, with a subset of patients showing clinical benefit from MEK inhibitors correlating with MAPK and interferon-related gene expressions. Spatial analysis revealed MAPK pathway activation localized to tumor boundaries, particularly in LGSC, underscoring the influence of the tumor microenvironment on treatment response.

Spatial analysis revealed MAPK pathway activation localized to tumor boundaries, particularly in LGSC, underscoring the influence of the tumor microenvironment on treatment response.

•FINPROVE (NCT05159245) is Finland’s first multi -center 
phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. 
•It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA -approved targeted 
therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who 
have exhausted standard treatments. 
•Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in 
gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper 
molecular insights. 
•Aim: To characterize the clinical, genomic, and 
transcriptomic features of gynecological cancer patients in 
FINPROVE and identify molecular characteristics 
associated with response to MEK -inhibitor therapy.METHODS
•From 1/2022 to 1/2026, 946 tumors were sequenced, including 131 (14%) 
gynecological cancers.
•A subset of tumors were analyzed with the NanoString pancancer IO 360 panel (770 
genes + 55 extra genes) and tissue cyclic immunofluorescence ( tCycIF , 25 antibodies)

•Aim: To characterize the clinical, genomic, and 
transcriptomic features of gynecological cancer patients in 
FINPROVE and identify molecular characteristics 
associated with response to MEK -inhibitor therapy.METHODS
•From 1/2022 to 1/2026, 946 tumors were sequenced, including 131 (14%) 
gynecological cancers.
•A subset of tumors were analyzed with the NanoString pancancer IO 360 panel (770 
genes + 55 extra genes) and tissue cyclic immunofluorescence ( tCycIF , 25 antibodies)

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/FINPROVE stitching & segmentation assessment notes.pdf
vector_chunks.text:
Stitching 
 
Kuvat powerpointissa 
Muuta huomioitavaa 
 
_p2 : adenocarcinoma vagina 
• Eka vs vika sykli muutamia irtonaisia soluja 
• Heilahtanut jossain kohdassa 
• Taustaa oikea ala kulma & markkerit napannut tähän voimakkaasti -> tod. näk. 
verisoluja ja verenvuotoa -> poista QC:ssä 
• Blurry spots vähän etenkin kanava 0 DAPI -> poista QC:ssä 
• Vasen ala kulma vasta -aine värjäys levinnyt reunoille & pakkautunut (?) -> ei. 
oikeita signaaleja? 
• Ala vasen tumor cells tuntuu ilmentävän itsessään immuunisolujen markkereita 
-> ei näytä immune infiltrationilta koska on tumor c ell tumia 
• PAX 8 ei toiminut -> cell phenotyping hyvin vaikeaa 
 
 
_p5: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat kohdillaan ok 
• Muutamia spotteja joissa yskittäiset solut ei ekassa ja vikassa DAPI syklissä 
kohdillaan 
• Blurry spot -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK ja pCREB värjäytyy lähinnä oikean puoleisissa tuumorisoluissa, ei ylä 
vase mmalla 
• pSTAT valotus ongelma 
 
 
_p11: HGSC uterus 
• Ei ihan kohdillaan mutta ok 
• Sykleissä ehkä taitteita -> poista QC:ssä -> käytä segmentointiin ehkä DAPI 3 
sykli mielummin? 
• Ei oikeestaan blurry spots

vector_chunks.text:
• DAPI 2 syklissä valotus ongelma 
• pCREB ja pERK ei aktiivisia tu umori soluissa ? 
• Ki67 kuitenkin super paljon tuumori soluissa ? 
• PSTAT valotus ongelma 
• Cyclin D löytyy signaalia, mutta outo spotti keskellä vasemmalla 
 
_p15 : LGSC ovary 
• Reunat ei täysin kohdillaan 
• Blurry spots aika paljon etenkin alaosassa kuvaa 
• Taitteita ylä ja alaosassa jonkin verran -> kaikki tulee poistaa 
 
_p21: HGSC ovary 
• Kohdillaan reunoilta ok hyvin 
• Oikealla blurry spot -> poista QC:ssä 
• DNA vähän blurry -> vaikea analysoida 
• Ei ole käytännössä ollenkaan tuumorisoluja eikä stroomasoluja -> vaikea 
analysoida mitään? 
• Super paljon CD138, CD31 ja CD163 
 
_p27: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat ihan ok kohdillaan 
• Vähän blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• Useita suonia ja niissä taustaa -> poista QC:ssä 
• CD138 co -localizes with panCK -> tumor pro bably expresses it self? 
 
_p32: LGSC ovary 
• Okealla alhaalla epätasainen kohta 
• Ei ihan kohdillaan reunoilta mutta ok 
• Verisuonissa markkerit kasautuu -> poista QC:ssä 
• Suurin osa blurry spoteista on tuumori solujen kohdalla -> voi olla vaikea 
analysoida, poista QC:ssä? 
 
_p41: LGSC ovary

vector_chunks.text:
• Vasemmalta ylhäältä puuttuu soluja ainakin vikasta syklistä 
• Ylhäällä mahdollisestii verta -> poista QC:ssä 
• Vasemmalla outo kohta, ei verta eikä nekroosia -> markkerit rakeisia tässä -> 
poista QC:ssä? 
• A lot of tumor looking nucleais that do not express panCK -> expresses Iba + 
vimentin -> what does it mean? Also the st aining of Iba looks a bit like fat tissue 
 
_p42: LGSC ovary 
• Ylhäältä ja alhaalta ei täsmää täysin 
• Vas ylhäällä epätasainen spot ti 
• Melko paljon blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä (eka vs vika sykli DAPI) 
• pERK not too much in the panCK cells 
 
_p44: LGSC ovary 
• Ainakin vikassa DAPIssa puuttuu suuri määrä soluja, näytteeseen on osuttu? -> 
optimize in segmentation? 
• Vähän blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK more on the edge of the tumor cells mostly 
 
_p45: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat ihan ok kohdillaan 
• Vikasta syklistä puuttuu soluja ja ekassa syklissä “solukasoja” 
• Paljon blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• Karva -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK on the edge of the tumor cells mostly 
 
_p54: endometrioid carcinoma ovary 
• Reunoilta ihan ok kohdillaan 
• Blurry spots ja tausta keräymiä -> poista QC:ssä 
• Ekan syklin DAPI heikoin -> käytä segmentoinnissa muuta kanavaa? 
• Useita tumor nucleais jotka ei ilmennä ollenkaan panCK:ta vaan vimentiiniä , 
ehkä käy läpi EMT? -> cell phenotyping tulee olemaan todella vaikeaa

vector_chunks.text:
_p74: HGSC ovary 
• Reunoilta ok kohdillaan 
• Vikasta syklistä puuttuu soluja 
• Blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• Ki_67 ja pSTAT illumnation problem 
• Nekroottisia kohtia -> poista QC:ssä 
 
 
_p75: mucinous carcinoma ovary 
• Reunat ei ihan kohdillaan 
• Ekassa syklissä taustaa ylhäällä -> poista QC:ssä 
• Blurry spotteja paljon -> poista QC:ssä 
• Ylempänä näytteessä epätasaisia kohtia -> poista QC:ssä 
• PanCK värjää kohtia joissa ei ole DAPIA -> liittyykö tapaan jolla näyte on leikattu/ 
tapa jolla solut järjestäytyy? - > vaikea cell phenotype ilman tumaa, membrane 
based segmentation?? 
• pERK lähinnä viementiinissä, ei panCK:ta ilmentävissä tuumorisoluissa 
 
_p78: HGSC ovary 
• Hyvin kohdillaan 
• Suhteellisen vähän blurry spots & muita epäselviä kohtia 
 
_p97: LGSC ovary 
• Hyvin kohdillaan 
• Blurry spots jonkun verran 
• Kohtia, joissa valtava määrä immuunisoluja päällekkäin -> voi olla vaikea 
segmentoida -> mesmer ? (tämä myös tärkeää tulosten kannalta koska 
luultavasti vastasi hyvin hoitoihin?) 
• Tissue folds -> poista QC:ssä 
• PERK lähinnä reunoilla tuumoria -> co localazes with cyclin D ? Ada huomannut 
cyclin D:llä

vector_chunks.text: Background: Important mediator of stress-related signaling. Is downstream of MKK’s, which in turn can be activated by members of the Ras family, leading to an additional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway. JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.

vector_chunks.text: Background: Important mediator of stress-related signaling. Is downstream of MKK’s, which in turn can be activated by members of the Ras family, leading to an additional signaling direction from the MAPK pathway. JNK signaling can inhibit apoptosis by preventing critical MOMP complex members from turning to their active, unphosphorylated form that is able to form the MOMP complex.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text:
Chosen targets circled in red.

Erk is the main kinase in the MAPK cascade, and in order to quantify the overall activity of the MAPK pathway we need to assess the level of Erk activity. Additionally, the ab is available directly conjugated, so we do not use up a non-conjugated 1st cycle slot with pErk.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text:
Chosen targets circled in red.

Erk is the main kinase in the MAPK cascade, and in order to quantify the overall activity of the MAPK pathway we need to assess the level of Erk activity. Additionally, the ab is available directly conjugated, so we do not use up a non-conjugated 1st cycle slot with pErk.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text: CREB2, i.e. ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g. PI3K-Akt-pathway. Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text: CREB2, i.e. ATF4 is a modulatory transcription factor downstream of Erk, but also downstream of e.g. PI3K-Akt-pathway. Quantifying CREB2 activity displays the level of MAPK kinase pathway activation, and when compared to level of Erk activation can give insights as to whether the activation of CREB2 is driven by Erk, or alternative pathways.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx
vector_chunks.text: JNK is a kinase downstream for Ras, i.e. activated by components upstream of MEKi, and leads to antiapoptotic signaling. Quantifying JNK activity can give insights as to why MEKi does not lead to improved survival, as cancer cells potentially rely on other pathways to survive instead of the canonical Erk driven MAPK pathway, activated by factors upstream of MEKi, and thus leave the cancer cells able to survive MEKi.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/33_FINPROVE MEKi cohort abs for tCycIF.docx

vector_chunks.text: Additional issue is whether we can use panCK and PAX8 as cancer markers for the whole cohort. We have one endometrial stromal sarcoma, and one vaginal ADC.

FINPROVE is Finland’s first multi-center, non-randomized phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA-approved targeted therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who have exhausted standard treatments. Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper molecular insight.

FINPROVE is Finland’s first multi-center, non-randomized phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA-approved targeted therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who have exhausted standard treatments. Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper molecular insight.

Between 1/2022 and 4/2025, tumors of 765 patients underwent targeted next-generation sequencing (NGS), of which 103 (13%) were gynecological cancers. NGS data were analyzed to identify potentially actionable alterations. A subset of tumors underwent further characterization using NanoString gene expression profiling and single-cell spatial proteomics. To date, 17 samples have been analyzed with tissue cyclic immunofluorescence (tCycIF) and 12 with NanoString nCounter profiling, aiming for a cohort of at least 36 patients.

Between 1/2022 and 4/2025, tumors of 765 patients underwent targeted next-generation sequencing (NGS), of which 103 (13%) were gynecological cancers. NGS data were analyzed to identify potentially actionable alterations. A subset of tumors underwent further characterization using NanoString gene expression profiling and single-cell spatial proteomics. To date, 17 samples have been analyzed with tissue cyclic immunofluorescence (tCycIF) and 12 with NanoString nCounter profiling, aiming for a cohort of at least 36 patients.

The most frequent gynecological cancers were high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC, n=28), low-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (LGSC, n=14) and uterine leiomyosarcoma (n=9). The most frequent targetable alterations were in KRAS (n=18) and BRAF (n=3). At data cutoff, 28 patients had received targeted treatment with a median treatment duration of 4 months. Among them, 1 complete response, 9 partial responses, 8 stable diseases, and 10 progressions were observed resulting in a disease control rate of 64% at 16 weeks, the trial’s primary endpoint. Preliminary NanoString data revealed differential MAPK pathway gene expression correlating with treatment responses. Moreover, expression of interferon-related genes correlated with response duration. The tCycIF analyses revealed distinct spatial MAPK pathway activation patterns across histologies, with a prominent activation on the tumor boundaries in LGSC.

The most frequent gynecological cancers were high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC, n=28), low-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (LGSC, n=14) and uterine leiomyosarcoma (n=9). The most frequent targetable alterations were in KRAS (n=18) and BRAF (n=3). At data cutoff, 28 patients had received targeted treatment with a median treatment duration of 4 months. Among them, 1 complete response, 9 partial responses, 8 stable diseases, and 10 progressions were observed resulting in a disease control rate of 64% at 16 weeks, the trial’s primary endpoint. Preliminary NanoString data revealed differential MAPK pathway gene expression correlating with treatment responses. Moreover, expression of interferon-related genes correlated with response duration. The tCycIF analyses revealed distinct spatial MAPK pathway activation patterns across histologies, with a prominent activation on the tumor boundaries in LGSC.

Frequent KRAS and BRAF alterations were identified in gynecological cancers, with a subset of patients showing clinical benefit from MEK inhibitors correlating with MAPK and interferon-related gene expressions. Spatial analysis revealed MAPK pathway activation localized to tumor boundaries, particularly in LGSC, underscoring the influence of the tumor microenvironment on treatment response.

Spatial analysis revealed MAPK pathway activation localized to tumor boundaries, particularly in LGSC, underscoring the influence of the tumor microenvironment on treatment response.

Nanostring panel & tCycIF imaging on patients with advanced gynecological malignancies in the FINPROVE clinical trial. Special focus on patients treated with MEK inhibitors & LGSC patients. Our aim is to discover correlations between transcriptional signatures, spatial protein expression and treatment responses.

vector_chunks.source_file: 33_FINPROVE_gyn_cancers/2. Methods & Experiments/tCycIF/Protocols/FINPROVE stitching & segmentation assessment notes.pdf
vector_chunks.text:
Stitching 
 
Kuvat powerpointissa 
Muuta huomioitavaa 
 
_p2 : adenocarcinoma vagina 
• Eka vs vika sykli muutamia irtonaisia soluja 
• Heilahtanut jossain kohdassa 
• Taustaa oikea ala kulma & markkerit napannut tähän voimakkaasti -> tod. näk. 
verisoluja ja verenvuotoa -> poista QC:ssä 
• Blurry spots vähän etenkin kanava 0 DAPI -> poista QC:ssä 
• Vasen ala kulma vasta -aine värjäys levinnyt reunoille & pakkautunut (?) -> ei. 
oikeita signaaleja? 
• Ala vasen tumor cells tuntuu ilmentävän itsessään immuunisolujen markkereita 
-> ei näytä immune infiltrationilta koska on tumor c ell tumia 
• PAX 8 ei toiminut -> cell phenotyping hyvin vaikeaa 
 
 
_p5: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat kohdillaan ok 
• Muutamia spotteja joissa yskittäiset solut ei ekassa ja vikassa DAPI syklissä 
kohdillaan 
• Blurry spot -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK ja pCREB värjäytyy lähinnä oikean puoleisissa tuumorisoluissa, ei ylä 
vase mmalla 
• pSTAT valotus ongelma 
 
 
_p11: HGSC uterus 
• Ei ihan kohdillaan mutta ok 
• Sykleissä ehkä taitteita -> poista QC:ssä -> käytä segmentointiin ehkä DAPI 3 
sykli mielummin? 
• Ei oikeestaan blurry spots

vector_chunks.text:
• DAPI 2 syklissä valotus ongelma 
• pCREB ja pERK ei aktiivisia tu umori soluissa ? 
• Ki67 kuitenkin super paljon tuumori soluissa ? 
• PSTAT valotus ongelma 
• Cyclin D löytyy signaalia, mutta outo spotti keskellä vasemmalla 
 
_p15 : LGSC ovary 
• Reunat ei täysin kohdillaan 
• Blurry spots aika paljon etenkin alaosassa kuvaa 
• Taitteita ylä ja alaosassa jonkin verran -> kaikki tulee poistaa 
 
_p21: HGSC ovary 
• Kohdillaan reunoilta ok hyvin 
• Oikealla blurry spot -> poista QC:ssä 
• DNA vähän blurry -> vaikea analysoida 
• Ei ole käytännössä ollenkaan tuumorisoluja eikä stroomasoluja -> vaikea 
analysoida mitään? 
• Super paljon CD138, CD31 ja CD163 
 
_p27: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat ihan ok kohdillaan 
• Vähän blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• Useita suonia ja niissä taustaa -> poista QC:ssä 
• CD138 co -localizes with panCK -> tumor pro bably expresses it self? 
 
_p32: LGSC ovary 
• Okealla alhaalla epätasainen kohta 
• Ei ihan kohdillaan reunoilta mutta ok 
• Verisuonissa markkerit kasautuu -> poista QC:ssä 
• Suurin osa blurry spoteista on tuumori solujen kohdalla -> voi olla vaikea 
analysoida, poista QC:ssä? 
 
_p41: LGSC ovary

vector_chunks.text:
• Vasemmalta ylhäältä puuttuu soluja ainakin vikasta syklistä 
• Ylhäällä mahdollisestii verta -> poista QC:ssä 
• Vasemmalla outo kohta, ei verta eikä nekroosia -> markkerit rakeisia tässä -> 
poista QC:ssä? 
• A lot of tumor looking nucleais that do not express panCK -> expresses Iba + 
vimentin -> what does it mean? Also the st aining of Iba looks a bit like fat tissue 
 
_p42: LGSC ovary 
• Ylhäältä ja alhaalta ei täsmää täysin 
• Vas ylhäällä epätasainen spot ti 
• Melko paljon blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä (eka vs vika sykli DAPI) 
• pERK not too much in the panCK cells 
 
_p44: LGSC ovary 
• Ainakin vikassa DAPIssa puuttuu suuri määrä soluja, näytteeseen on osuttu? -> 
optimize in segmentation? 
• Vähän blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK more on the edge of the tumor cells mostly 
 
_p45: LGSC ovary 
• Reunat ihan ok kohdillaan 
• Vikasta syklistä puuttuu soluja ja ekassa syklissä “solukasoja” 
• Paljon blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• Karva -> poista QC:ssä 
• pERK on the edge of the tumor cells mostly 
 
_p54: endometrioid carcinoma ovary 
• Reunoilta ihan ok kohdillaan 
• Blurry spots ja tausta keräymiä -> poista QC:ssä 
• Ekan syklin DAPI heikoin -> käytä segmentoinnissa muuta kanavaa? 
• Useita tumor nucleais jotka ei ilmennä ollenkaan panCK:ta vaan vimentiiniä , 
ehkä käy läpi EMT? -> cell phenotyping tulee olemaan todella vaikeaa

_p74: HGSC ovary 
• Reunoilta ok kohdillaan 
• Vikasta syklistä puuttuu soluja 
• Blurry spots -> poista QC:ssä 
• Ki_67 ja pSTAT illumnation problem 
• Nekroottisia kohtia -> poista QC:ssä 
 
 
_p75: mucinous carcinoma ovary 
• Reunat ei ihan kohdillaan 
• Ekassa syklissä taustaa ylhäällä -> poista QC:ssä 
• Blurry spotteja paljon -> poista QC:ssä 
• Ylempänä näytteessä epätasaisia kohtia -> poista QC:ssä 
• PanCK värjää kohtia joissa ei ole DAPIA -> liittyykö tapaan jolla näyte on leikattu/ 
tapa jolla solut järjestäytyy? - > vaikea cell phenotype ilman tumaa, membrane 
based segmentation?? 
• pERK lähinnä viementiinissä, ei panCK:ta ilmentävissä tuumorisoluissa

• Hyvin kohdillaan 
• Suhteellisen vähän blurry spots & muita epäselviä kohtia

• Blurry spots jonkun verran 
• Kohtia, joissa valtava määrä immuunisoluja päällekkäin -> voi olla vaikea 
segmentoida -> mesmer ? (tämä myös tärkeää tulosten kannalta koska 
luultavasti vastasi hyvin hoitoihin?) 
• Tissue folds -> poista QC:ssä 
• PERK lähinnä reunoilla tuumoria -> co localazes with cyclin D ? Ada huomannut 
cyclin D:llä

CONTACT INFORMATION
anni.suoknuuti@helsinki.fiCONCLUSIONS
MEK inhibitors were the most commonly used targeted therapy and showed the highest response rates in low -grade serous ovarian ca rcinoma, 
where tumors shared mutation -independent MAPK -driven transcriptional programs, while poor response and targetable mutations were
associated with enhanced inflammatory and interferon -related gene expression.INTRODUCTION
•FINPROVE (NCT05159245) is Finland’s first multi -center 
phase II clinical trial advancing precision cancer medicine. 
•It evaluates the efficacy of EMA/FDA -approved targeted 
therapies based on tumor molecular profiles in patients who 
have exhausted standard treatments. 
•Despite shared genomic alterations, treatment responses in 
gynecological cancers vary, highlighting the need for deeper 
molecular insights. 
•Aim: To characterize the clinical, genomic, and 
transcriptomic features of gynecological cancer patients in 
FINPROVE and identify molecular characteristics 
associated with response to MEK -inhibitor therapy.METHODS
•From 1/2022 to 1/2026, 946 tumors were sequenced, including 131 (14%) 
gynecological cancers.
•A subset of tumors were analyzed with the NanoString pancancer IO 360 panel (770 
genes + 55 extra genes) and tissue cyclic immunofluorescence ( tCycIF , 25 antibodies)
1.Clinical & genomic characterization

most common tumour histology.3. The most frequently altered gene among gynecological
cancer patients was TP53 , followed by KRAS and PIK3CA.
4. TP53 mutations associated with CCNE1 and MYC amplification.
5. Among gynecological cancer patients in FINPROVE, MEK
